heading: Replacement fuel supply and demand program
label: 13252 Replacement fuel supply and demand program

children.heading: Establishment of program
children.label: a Establishment of program
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program to promote the development and use in light duty motor vehicles of domestic replacement fuels. Such program shall promote the replacement of petroleum motor fuels with replacement fuels to the maximum extent practicable. Such program shall, to the extent practicable, ensure the availability of those replacement fuels that will have the greatest impact in reducing oil imports, improving the health of our Nation’s economy and reducing greenhouse gas emissions.

children.heading: Development plan and production goals
children.label: b Development plan and production goals

children.text: estimate the domestic and nondomestic production capacity for replacement fuels and alternative fueled vehicles needed to implement this section;

children.label: A
children.text: at least 10 percent by the year 2000; and

children.label: B
children.text: at least 30 percent by the year 2010,

determine the most suitable means and methods of developing and encouraging the production, distribution, and use of replacement fuels and alternative fueled vehicles in a manner that would meet the program goals described in subsection (a);

identify ways to encourage the development of reliable replacement fuels and alternative fueled vehicle industries in the United States, and the technical, economic, and institutional barriers to such development; and

determine the greenhouse gas emission implications of increasing the use of replacement fuels, including an estimate of the maximum feasible reduction in such emissions from the use of replacement fuels.
